description_long: This is a vintage, freestanding kitchen hutch cabinet with a distressed green paint finish and visible signs of wear and age. The upper section features two large cabinet doors with metal handles, while the lower section has two additional doors and a pair of drawers, all with matching handles. The central area between the upper and lower cabinets is recessed, providing an open shelf space for easy access or display. The cabinet's surfaces show patches of worn paint, especially on the edges and corners, revealing a darker underlayer. The back of the cabinet is solid, with no openings, and the sides are plain except for the visible wear. The overall construction suggests it is made primarily of wood with metal hardware, suitable for storage in urban environments such as garages, workshops, or older kitchens.
description: A distressed green vintage kitchen hutch cabinet with upper and lower doors, drawers, and a central open shelf. The cabinet features worn paint, metal handles, and a sturdy wooden build.
description_view_0: Front view showing two upper and two lower cabinet doors, two drawers, and an open central shelf with visible handles.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the cabinet's depth, plain side panel, and the profile of the open shelf area.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a solid back panel with visible paint wear and the open lower shelf compartments.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the cabinet's profile and the layered construction.
